title: Ring Buffer
prompt:
Write a Hemlock program implementing a ring buffer (circular buffer) for i32 values using raw memory allocation.

Implement:
1. `ring_new(capacity)` - allocates memory for `capacity` i32s (4 bytes each), returns object {ptr, capacity, head: 0, tail: 0, count: 0}
2. `ring_push(ring, value)` - writes value at tail position, advances tail (wrapping), returns true. If full, returns false.
3. `ring_pop(ring)` - reads value at head position, advances head (wrapping), returns the value. If empty, returns null.
4. `ring_free(ring)` - frees the underlying memory

Use ptr_write_i32/ptr_deref_i32 with byte offsets (index * 4). Use modulo for wrapping.

Test with capacity 4:
- Push 10, 20, 30, 40 (all succeed) - print "pushed: 10 20 30 40"
- Push 50 (full, fails) - print "full: true"
- Pop twice - print "popped: 10 20"
- Push 50, 60 (now succeeds) - print "pushed: 50 60"
- Pop all remaining - print "popped: 30 40 50 60"
- Pop from empty - print "empty: true"
- Free the ring buffer
expected_output:
pushed: 10 20 30 40
full: true
popped: 10 20
pushed: 50 60
popped: 30 40 50 60
empty: true
